findings.evidence: Mathlib provides the coalgebra-level API: `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/GroupLike.lean:37` defines `IsGroupLikeElem` from counit/comul, and `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/GroupLike.lean:27` proves `IsGroupLikeElem.one`; the PR’s `instBase` at lines 57-64 only proves coassoc/counit from `1` being grouplike.
findings.fix: Generalize the primary construction to `[AddCommMonoid C] [Module R C] [Coalgebra R C] (g : GroupLike R C)` with coaction `r ↦ r ⊗ (g : C)`, then define the current bialgebra trivial object as the specialization `g = (1 : GroupLike R C)` using `IsGroupLikeElem.one`.
findings.issue: The whole trivial-comodule section assumes `[Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]`, but the coaction `r ↦ r ⊗ g`, `IsCoinvariant`, and `ofCoinvariant` only need a coalgebra with a chosen grouplike element `g`; the bialgebra multiplication is only used to know `1` is grouplike.

summary: The new API is stated at bialgebra level, but most of the construction naturally lives at the existing coalgebra-plus-grouplike level. Prove the grouplike/coaugmented version first and derive the bialgebra `1` special case from `IsGroupLikeElem.one`.
